description_long: This object is a rustic, stone-based lantern with a cylindrical body and a wide, circular base that appears to be constructed from rough-hewn stone blocks. The upper section features a wooden frame supporting a pitched roof made of reddish-brown tiles, reminiscent of traditional well or gazebo roofs. The roof is slightly curved and overhangs the stone body, providing shelter to the central opening. A horizontal wooden spindle with handles on both sides is mounted above the opening, suggesting a mechanism for lowering or raising an internal element, though no light source is visible. The overall design is robust and weathered, suitable for outdoor urban environments such as parks or plazas. The lantern's materials and construction evoke a historical or old-world aesthetic, blending stone and wood with a touch of brickwork on the roof.
description: A rustic stone and wood lantern with a cylindrical stone base, overhanging tiled roof, and a central spindle mechanism. The design is robust and weathered, evoking a historical outdoor aesthetic.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden spindle and the pitched, tiled roof with the stone base clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curved roof tiles and the cylindrical stone body, with the spindle handle visible on the left.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the spindle and the overhanging roof, but with less detail on the spindle mechanism.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the spindle handle on the right, emphasizing the brick-patterned roof and stone base.
